title: Gomphrena pilosa
intro: Gomphrena pilosa is a type of flowering plant, commonly known as the 'False Bugloss' or 'Indian Alkanet', belonging to the Amaranthaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and its roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gomphrena pilosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Gomphrena pilosa is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of dried roots, capsules, and tinctures to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Gomphrena pilosa is classified within the family Amaranthaceae. This species belongs to the genus Gomphrena, which comprises several species of flowering plants. The classification of Gomphrena pilosa is further divided into the order Caryophyllales, followed by the class Magnoliopsida. Gomphrena pilosa is a type of flowering plant that is native to tropical regions. Its classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships between different plant species.
